name: ExtensionElementDefinition_ValueAlternatives
title: R5: Extensions that are allowed to replace a primitive value (new)
purpose:
This extension is part of the cross-version definitions generated to enable use of the
element `ElementDefinition.valueAlternatives` as defined in FHIR R5
in FHIR R4.

The source element is defined as:
`ElementDefinition.valueAlternatives` 0..* `canonical(http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/StructureDefinition)`

Across FHIR versions, the element set has been mapped as:
*  R5: `ElementDefinition.valueAlternatives` 0..* `canonical(http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/StructureDefinition)`

Following are the generation technical comments:
Element `ElementDefinition.valueAlternatives` has a context of ElementDefinition based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `ElementDefinition`.
Element `ElementDefinition.valueAlternatives` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.short: R5: Extensions that are allowed to replace a primitive value (new)
element.comment:
Element `ElementDefinition.valueAlternatives` has a context of ElementDefinition based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `ElementDefinition`.
Element `ElementDefinition.valueAlternatives` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).
if mustHaveValue is true, then this element should not be present, since no extensions are allowed in place of the value. Note that this element has no impact if the value is present.

element.short: Extensions that are allowed to replace a primitive value
element.definition: Specifies a list of extensions that can appear in place of a primitive value.
element.comment: if mustHaveValue is true, then this element should not be present, since no extensions are allowed in place of the value. Note that this element has no impact if the value is present.
